commit.message:
feat(governance): refactor guardrails + fix product card clickability (#462)

* add refactor governance guardrails

* fix(products): make product card fully clickable on desktop

Card article had no click handler — navigation only worked via child
nuxt-links (image area + product name). Clicks on category text or
description didn't navigate, causing the critical E2E test to fail.

- Add handleCardClick on <article> that calls router.push, skipping
  clicks already handled by <a>/<button> children
- Move useRouter() to setup-level (was incorrectly called inside onTap
  callback, violating Vue composition API rules)
- Add cursor-pointer to article for correct UX affordance

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test(product-card): add useRouter mock to Card unit tests

useRouter is now called at setup level in Card.vue — the #imports mock
needed the stub to prevent setup errors in Vitest.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test(critical): wait for networkidle before clicking product card

waitForSelector fires on SSR-rendered DOM before Vue hydration,
so the @click handler on the article wasn't attached yet. Using
waitForLoadState('networkidle') matches the pattern used in the
passing products-page test and ensures Vue event handlers are bound.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(product-card): move onUnmounted to setup level

onUnmounted was called inside onMounted (inside setupMobileTouch),
which is undefined behavior — Vue lifecycle hooks must be called
synchronously during component setup. Replaced with a module-level
cleanup ref assigned in onMounted, released in a top-level onUnmounted.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement audit issues 1-7: crew split, waterfall fixes, validation, and analysis

Issue 1: Add detailed crew compositions (CrewCompositions.js) with labor/equipment
breakdowns for all 13 crews, enabling accurate L/E cost split throughout the waterfall.

Issues 2+3: Route cluster mobilization and safety crew cost into the cost waterfall.
When cluster mode is active, cluster mob replaces activity-level mob to avoid
double-counting. Safety cost (roadway mode) is now additive to direct cost.

Issue 4: Add fee/profit bounds checking — error >50%, warning >25%, info >20%.
New analysis rule flags atypical markup with corrective suggestions.

Issue 5: Link confidence score to contingency recommendation via AACE estimate
class ranges. LOW confidence maps to upper contingency range, HIGH to lower.
Validator warns when actual contingency falls outside recommended bounds.

Issue 6: Enhance benchmark flags with component-level diagnosis — identifies
the dominant cost driver (labor/equipment/material/trucking) and suggests
specific corrective actions instead of generic "review" messages.

Issue 7: Add output integrity checks — sum verification, component allocation,
non-negative invariants, waterfall continuity, and duration-production consistency.

commit.message:
Add configurable lognormal forcing and CV borrowing for small samples

Implements PI decision: "For any n < 5, borrow the CV directly from the median
of all samples with >5"

Key Changes:
1. Added FORCE_LOGNORMAL config flag in distribution_fitting.py (line 44)
   - Set to True: forces lognormal for all fits (where feasible)
   - Set to False: uses AIC-based selection (lognormal/gamma/truncated_normal/uniform)
   - Easily changeable for future experiments

2. Implemented CV borrowing in src/cv_borrowing.py
   - Computes median CV from all lognormal pairs with n > 5
   - Applies borrowed CV to lognormal pairs with n < 5
   - Preserves median intensity while adjusting sigma parameter
   - Integrated into main simulation pipeline (stock_flow_simulation.py)

3. Created distribution fit comparison visualizations
   - diagnostics/compare_distribution_fits.py: generates 4-panel plots
   - Shows histogram+PDFs, CDFs, Q-Q plots, fit quality metrics
   - 12 visualizations for highest-n pairs to assess fit quality

Results with FORCE_LOGNORMAL = True:
- Lognormal: 25 pairs (15%) - 19 with n>5, 6 with n<5
- Uniform: 142 pairs (85%) - fallback for n=1 and fitting failures
- Reference CV: 0.671 (median from 19 well-characterized pairs)
- All 6 lognormal pairs with n<5 now use borrowed CV = 0.671

Testing:
- diagnostics/test_cv_borrowing.py: validates CV borrowing implementation
- diagnostics/check_distribution_types_by_n.py: analyzes distribution selection by sample size

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: setup gate bypassed by stale session state across sessions

The UserPromptSubmit setup gate cached setup_confirmed in session state
as a fast-path optimization. Since CLAUDE_SESSION_ID falls back to
"default" and the session state file in TMPDIR persists across sessions,
stale setup_confirmed=True from a previous session let prompts through
even when config.json was missing.

Root cause: premature optimization — caching a sub-millisecond file
check (Path.exists + json.loads) in session state introduced a whole
class of stale-state bugs.

Fix:
- prompt_submit.py: Remove session state sentinel caching. Always check
  config.json on disk — the single source of truth for setup status.
- bootstrap.py: Detect stale session files (session_ended=True) and
  delete them at session start. Remove setup_confirmed sentinel writes.
- Remove _build_onboarding_directive and onboarding_directive variable
  from prompt_submit since the gate now only hard-blocks or passes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix file_renamer base directory detection for Linux paths

Replace os.path.splitdrive-based logic with finding "Culture/Videos" in the existing file path. The old approach produced /Culture/Videos on Linux (empty drive letter), but the actual mount point is something like /mnt/w/Culture/Videos.

  questions with context from sellerNotes and suggests more product Q&A;
  round-0 reply suggestions also switched to product questions

- Navbar: updated accent colors to match Ocean Dark
- AuthContext + auth.js: firebase-sync endpoint returns avatar from MongoDB

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: PEIMS export pre-flight data quality check

Adds a "Data Quality Pre-Flight Check" panel to the PEIMS Export tab.
On demand (click "Run Check"), queries the current school year's
approved/active/completed incidents for three critical gaps:
  - Missing offense code
  - Missing incident date
  - Missing consequence type

Shows a green pass or an amber breakdown by issue type. TEA rejects
rows with missing required fields — this catches problems before download.

- Render deck pile as stacked card backs (right side, shifted left+down per layer)
- Fix DrawTexturePro rotation: dest.x/y is the pivot; origin offsets card center
- Procedural card back with rounded rect, dark blue fill and inset border
- Drag: hold LMB on a player card to pick it up enlarged, release to return it
- Hand cursor when hovering or dragging a player card

commit.message:
🐛 fix: move workflows outside commands/ to prevent them showing as slash commands

Claude Code scans .claude/commands/ recursively and treats every .md
file as a slash command. Workflows are internal implementation details
and should not be user-invocable.

Workflows now install to .claude/clancy/workflows/ (local) or
~/.claude/clancy/workflows/ (global) — outside of commands/.
Updated all @ references in command files accordingly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
